instructions.runTests: Tap the play button to run all tests.
instructions.discovery: Use the Network Discovery card to scan for devices on your network.

tooltips.linkStatus: Shows the physical link status and connection properties
tooltips.networkConfig: Displays IP configuration and DHCP information
tooltips.discovery: Scans your network to find connected devices
tooltips.gateway: Shows default gateway and connectivity status
tooltips.publicIp: Displays your public IP address and location
tooltips.dns: Tests DNS resolution and shows configured servers
tooltips.wifi: Shows WiFi connection details and signal strength
tooltips.cable: Tests Ethernet cable integrity and length

settings.theme: Choose between light, dark, or system theme
settings.interface: Select the network interface to monitor
settings.thresholds: Set warning and critical threshold values

about.description: The Seed is a comprehensive network diagnostics and monitoring tool by Mustard Seed Networks, designed to provide deep visibility into your network infrastructure.

realTimeMonitoring.title: Real-time Monitoring
realTimeMonitoring.description: Monitor link status, WiFi signal strength, gateway reachability, and more with live updates.

networkDiscovery.title: Network Discovery
networkDiscovery.description: Discover all devices on your network with ARP scanning, LLDP/CDP neighbor detection.

performanceTesting.title: Performance Testing
performanceTesting.description: Run internet speed tests and LAN throughput tests with iperf3 integration.

cableDiagnostics.title: Cable Diagnostics
cableDiagnostics.description: Test Ethernet cables using Time Domain Reflectometry (TDR) to find faults and measure length.

openSource.title: Open Source & Customizable
openSource.description: The Seed is open source software. Customize thresholds, configure tests, and integrate with your existing monitoring infrastructure.

dashboard.title: Dashboard Overview
dashboard.description: The main dashboard shows all diagnostic cards. Each card displays real-time information about a specific aspect of your network.

interface.title: Select Network Interface
interface.description: Use the interface dropdown in the header to select which network interface to monitor (e.g., eth0, wlan0). Click "Settings" to configure interface-specific options.

thresholds.title: Configure Thresholds
thresholds.description: Open Settings → Thresholds to customize warning and critical levels for DNS latency, gateway ping, WiFi signal strength, and more.

runTests.title: Run Tests
runTests.description: Click the "Run All Tests" button (⚡) in the bottom right to execute speed tests, cable diagnostics, network discovery, and custom health checks.

exploreCards.title: Explore Individual Cards
exploreCards.description: Each card has a help icon (?) that explains the metrics shown. Click on cards to view detailed information and run specific tests.

proTips.title: Pro Tips
tips: Use the Network Discovery card to find all devices on your network
Set up custom health check tests in Settings → Custom Tests
Export diagnostics data for troubleshooting or documentation
Enable auto-scan for continuous network monitoring

linkStatus.description: Monitors the physical layer connection of your network interface.

carrier.description: Physical layer signal detection. Shows 'Connected' when NIC detects a link partner (cable connected to active port).

speed.description: Negotiated link speed between your network interface and the connected device (e.g., 1000 Mbps).

duplex.description: Communication mode - Full duplex allows simultaneous bidirectional data, Half duplex is one direction at a time.

autoNeg.description: Whether speed and duplex were auto-negotiated with the link partner or manually configured.

mtu.description: Maximum Transmission Unit - largest packet size (in bytes) that can be sent without fragmentation. Standard is 1500 bytes.

cableTest.description: Time Domain Reflectometry tests cable quality and detects faults.

tdrTest.description: Sends electrical pulses through the cable and measures reflections to detect faults and measure length.

cableStatus.description: Shows if cable pairs are OK, open (disconnected), short (wires touching), or have impedance mismatch.

faultDistance.description: Distance to cable fault in meters. Helps locate physical cable problems.

pairs.description: Ethernet cables have 4 twisted pairs. Gigabit uses all 4; Fast Ethernet uses pairs 1-2 and 3-6.

cableTest.note: Cable testing requires compatible network hardware. Not all NICs support TDR.

wifiStatus.description: Monitor wireless connection quality and settings.

ssid.description: Service Set Identifier - the name of the wireless network you're connected to.

bssid.description: Basic Service Set Identifier - MAC address of the access point.

signal.description: Signal strength in dBm. -30 is excellent, -67 is good, -70 is fair, -80 is weak. Higher (less negative) is better.

channel.description: WiFi channel number (1-14 for 2.4GHz, 36-165 for 5GHz). Overlapping channels cause interference.

security.description: Encryption protocol protecting the connection (WPA2, WPA3, WEP, or Open).

frequency.description: Radio band - 2.4GHz has better range, 5GHz has better speed and less interference.

networkDhcp.description: Shows IP configuration and DHCP lease information.

leaseTime.description: Duration of current IP address assignment before renewal is needed.

dhcpServer.description: IP address of the DHCP server that issued the lease (usually your router).

gateway.description: Default gateway assigned by DHCP for routing traffic off-subnet.

dnsServers.description: DNS servers assigned by DHCP for name resolution.

subnetMask.description: Network mask defining the local subnet size.

gatewayHelp.description: Tests reachability and latency to your default gateway.

ipv4Gateway.description: Default router for IPv4 traffic leaving your local network.

ipv6Gateway.description: Default router for IPv6 traffic (may be link-local address).

reachability.description: Whether the gateway responds to ICMP ping requests.

latency.description: Round-trip time to gateway. Should be <1ms for local networks.

packetLoss.description: Percentage of ping packets that didn't receive a response.

dnsTests.description: Tests DNS resolution performance and functionality.

forwardLookup.description: Resolves hostname to IPv4 address (A record).

reverseLookup.description: Resolves IP address back to hostname (PTR record).

ipv6Lookup.description: Resolves hostname to IPv6 address (AAAA record).

latency.description: Time taken for the DNS query to complete. Good: <50ms for local DNS.

performanceTests.description: Run speed tests to measure network throughput.

internetSpeed.description: Tests download/upload speeds to public speedtest servers. Measures your connection to the internet.

lanSpeed.description: Tests throughput on your local network using iperf3 to a configured server.

download.description: Maximum download speed achieved during the test.

upload.description: Maximum upload speed achieved during the test.

latency.description: Round-trip time (ping) to the test server.

jitter.description: Variation in latency over time. Lower is better for real-time applications like VoIP and gaming.

networkDiscovery.description: Discover all devices on your network and identify connected switches.

networkScan.description: Discovers active devices on your network subnet using ARP/ICMP ping sweeps.

macAddress.description: Hardware address of the network interface - unique identifier for the device.

vendor.description: Manufacturer identified from the MAC address OUI (first 3 bytes).

hostname.description: DNS hostname if reverse lookup succeeded.

lldpCdp.description: Link Layer Discovery Protocol (standard) or Cisco Discovery Protocol - provides information about directly connected network switches.
